name: phonopy (Phonon Calculations for Materials Science)

performance.notes: NumPy-based; force constant calculations depend on DFT code speed

agent_readiness.notes: Phonon calculations for materials science. from phonopy import Phonopy. phonon = Phonopy(unitcell, supercell_matrix=[[2,0,0],[0,2,0],[0,0,2]]). phonon.generate_displacements(distance=0.01). supercells = phonon.supercells_with_displacements. [run DFT on each supercell]. phonon.forces = forces. phonon.produce_force_constants(). phonon.run_mesh([8,8,8]). phonon.run_thermal_properties(t_step=10, t_max=1000). phonon.plot_band_structure(). phonon.plot_dos(). phono3py for thermal conductivity. VASP, GPAW, QE, LAMMPS interfaces.

gotchas: Requires external DFT code (VASP, GPAW, QE) for force calculations
Supercell approach requires understanding of phonon theory
Force symmetrization is important — can affect results
phono3py (thermal conductivity) is separate package with more complexity
best_when: Thermal properties (Cv, entropy, Helmholtz free energy) from phonons
When VASP/QE/GPAW force calculations are available
summary: phonopy calculates phonon properties of crystals. Phonopy(unitcell, supercell_matrix). generate_displacements(). Run DFT → set forces → produce_force_constants(). run_mesh(). run_thermal_properties(). plot_band_structure(). BSD-3 by Atsushi Togo. Standard for phonon dispersion and thermal properties. Requires external DFT code for forces. phono3py extension for thermal conductivity.
